do_not_do: Do not delete project S00-S19 before repository source delivery is proven.
Do not score any run that may mix project-upload and repository source content.
Do not treat immutable knowledge/base as the permanently active model.
Do not claim prediction improvement from validators or interface patches alone.
Do not silently fall back to project uploads when repository retrieval fails.

input: exact repository release tag or commit
active S00-S19 manifest and S19 binding table
output: all required source families or explicit not-applicable reasons
exact library IDs and canonical paths
parent-clause byte ranges and excerpt hashes
conditions, limitations, negations, exceptions and capability ceilings
required_repository_source_delivery.chat_reasoning_requirement: The ChatGPT session may cite and use only packet items from the exact repository release.
required_repository_source_delivery.validation_requirement: Every evidence-ledger row must resolve to a packet item and bound repository hash; project-upload references cause failure.

cutover_phases.action: Keep current project files as read-only bootstrap; suspend scored retraining because source delivery is not yet causal.
Build candidate/release directories, active release manifest, chunked locator objects and SOURCE-PACKET-BUILDER-V1.
Run a byte-identical R16 shadow test: repository packets only, project uploads explicitly unused, and compare source/evidence coverage.
Materialize the R21 revised candidate S00-S19 and run the same 25 questions using repository packets only.

After source-authority and clean-retest validation passes, create a new clean ChatGPT project containing the active main prompt and GitHub access but no S00-S19 uploads; preserve the old project as an archive.
All later training creates new repository candidates/releases; the clean runtime project never stores duplicate S00-S19 files.

current_training_claim.reason: No revised complete S00-S19 release has yet been causally delivered to an answer-isolated prediction session.
